title: myriopteris clevelandii
intro: Myriopteris clevelandii, also known as the Cleveland's lip fern or Cleveland's oak fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, particularly in the states of Alabama, Georgia, and South Carolina. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as arthritis, fever, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Myriopteris clevelandii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and wellness by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and boosting the immune system.
